subject: Physics
title: Lightning Is Static Electricity
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Lightning Is Static Electricity.

hook.text: Lightning is just static electricity on a massive scale—the same spark you feel touching a doorknob.

buildup.text: Inside storm clouds, ice crystals collide and separate charges. The top goes positive, the bottom negative.

discovery.text: When the charge difference between cloud and ground becomes enormous, air breaks down and current flows—lightning.

twist.text: A lightning bolt is hotter than the Sun's surface and carries up to 300 million volts.

climax.text: Earth is struck by lightning about 100 times every second—8.6 million bolts per day.

punchline.text: Same physics as a doorknob zap. Just a billion times more powerful.

quiz.question: How does charge separation occur in storm clouds?
options: Ice crystals collide and separate positive and negative charges
Raindrops absorb electricity from the ground
Wind generates electricity through friction with air molecules
